scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verifies that parse_exchange_file correctly handles TSV files with standard and swapped column order, YAML files, and files without recognizable extension (content sniffing).
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Prove that parse_exchange_file can detect format by content and handle arbitrary header column ordering.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When given a TSV file with columns in order 'lower, compound, upper', the function must correctly map 'compound' to the second column.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Seed solution (extension-based, fixed order) would incorrectly parse such a file, e.g., producing entries with compound='-10' and lower='Glucose'.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
tsv_path = 'exchange_limits.tsv'
with open(tsv_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('Glucose\t-10\t100\n')
    f.write('Lactate\t0\t50\n')
yaml_path = 'exchange_limits.yaml'
with open(yaml_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('Glucose:-10:100:cytosol\n')
    f.write('O2:-1000:1000:mitochondria\n')
swapped_path = 'exchange_limits_swapped.tsv'
with open(swapped_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('lower\tcompound\tupper\n')
    f.write('-10\tGlucose\t100\n')
    f.write('0\tLactate\t50\n')
noext_path = 'no_ext'
with open(noext_path, 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('Glucose\t-10\t100\n')
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must verify that parse_exchange_file correctly parses TSV files with arbitrary column order and files without recognizable extension.
